# Dock dockPreview.v1 producer + unit test (compute half)

## Context

The functional cross-zone tab drag landed (#14857 / PR #14864): `DockTabSortZone` fires `dockCrossZoneDrop` on drop, and the owner reducer hit-tests the zone under the pointer and commits a semantic `moveItem`. That slice commits the model move **directly** from the hit-test — it does not yet produce a `neo.harness.dockPreview.v1` object, render the drag-time hover affordance, or use `DockPreview.previewToOperation`. This leaf builds those on the landed scaffold.

## The Problem

Two gaps remain from the full drop-zone producer contract:
1. **No `dockPreview.v1` production.** The reducer maps `(pointer, rendered zone rects) → targetNodeId → moveItem` directly. The ADR-0029 preview→operation model wants `(pointer, rects) → dockPreview.v1{placement} → previewToOperation → descriptor`. Producing the preview object is also what `#14769`'s `CrossWindowDragTarget.previewFor` seam binds to — until it exists, `#14769` has no landed compute path (see its blocked-by note).
2. **No drag-time hover affordance.** `AgentOS.view.DockPreview` (the built renderer) is wired nowhere; there is no visual band/guide showing WHERE the tab will land while dragging. And only `tab-into` placement is handled — `edge-*` / `split-*` (drop-on-edge → new split) are unhandled.

## The Fix

On drag-move, hit-test the workspace → produce a `dockPreview.v1` with the resolved `placement.kind` (`tab-into` today; add `edge-{top,right,bottom,left}` / `split-{before,after}`); feed `DockPreview` to render the transient affordance; on drop, convert via the landed `DockPreview.previewToOperation()` → `applyDockZoneOperation()` (replacing the direct-`moveItem` shortcut in `MainContainer.onDockCrossZoneDrop`). Add a **unit** test for the hit-test → `placement.kind` mapping (pure `(rects, pointer) → dockPreview.v1`), which the model-move slice deferred.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hit-test emits a schema-valid `neo.harness.dockPreview.v1` with the correct `placement.kind` for `tab-into` + `edge-*` + `split-*` — unit-tested against known rects.
- [ ] `DockPreview` renders the affordance during a live drag (hover band/guide), cleared fail-closed on a `rejected` placement.
- [ ] Drop routes through `previewToOperation` → `applyDockZoneOperation` (the direct-`moveItem` shortcut retired); the `DockCrossZoneDragNL` e2e stays green + gains an edge/split case.
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope

Cross-window transfer (`#14769`, which this unblocks) · grouped-node drag (`#14770`).

## Related

Parent `#13158` · builds on `#14857` (the landed cross-zone scaffold) · **unblocks `#14769`** (its `previewFor` seam) · renderer `apps/agentos/view/DockPreview.mjs` · authority ADR 0029 §2.3.

- 2026-07-05T20:10:22Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `774e409` - "feat(dashboard): dock cross-zone tab drag — tab-into model move + green e2e (#14857)

Releasing a tab header into a DIFFERENT dock zone now relocates the item in the
committed dockZone.v1 document — the gesture beyond #14850's within-container reorder.
This slice commits the model move DIRECTLY from the pointer hit-test; the dockPreview.v1
producer + DockPreview hover rendering + edge/split placements are the #14866 follow-up.

Reuse-and-route, no parallel drag system: a dock-aware tab-header SortZone
(DockTabSortZone) rides the existing drag lifecycle and, on drop, fires a
dockCrossZoneDrop event on its tab.Container. The adapter wires the listener beside
the within-container moveTo one; its closure holds the reducer (captured at projection
time — the reliable seam, since Neo's config clone strips functions and the owner id is
unset at projection). The reducer hit-tests the zone under the pointer and commits a
semantic moveItem; a same-zone drop is a no-op. project() threads onDockCrossZoneDrop
through the context.

Ships its whitebox-e2e green in the same PR (gesture-proof guardrail):
DockCrossZoneDragNL drags Strategy out of main-tabs onto the Terminal zone and asserts
App-Worker truth — strategy moves main-tabs -> terminal-tabs. The within-container
journey (DockDragDropNL) stays green; 123 dock unit specs pass."

- 2026-07-06T09:57:48Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #14880
- 2026-07-06T10:05:20Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `0bf0625` - "feat(dashboard): dockPreview.v1 producer (config-tunable geometry) + unit test (#14866)

The COMPUTE half of the preview->operation pipeline: (pointer, zone rects) -> neo.harness.dockPreview.v1, the owner callback for CrossWindowDragTarget.previewFor/hitTest. Layer-blind (src/dashboard cannot import the app-layer DockPreview) — the producer->consumer contract pins in the unit test via DockPreview.isValidPreview.

Instance + non-reactive configs (schema, edgeBandRatio), NOT static fields: the geometry is tunable via Neo.overwrites / per-instance / subclass and resolvePlacementKind is overridable — the core.Base customization surface, per operator review.

6/6 unit green: 5-zone hit-test (tab-into + edge-*), fail-closed, innermost hitTestZone, config customization, produce->isValidPreview pin, deterministic previewId.

WIP: split-* (needs adapter zone-tree/orientation), renderer wiring + e2e follow on this PR."
- 2026-07-06T10:12:25Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `c0f6c24` - "feat(dashboard): dockPreview.v1 producer (config-tunable, all placement kinds) + unit test (#14866)

The COMPUTE half of the preview->operation pipeline: (pointer, zone rects) -> neo.harness.dockPreview.v1, the owner callback for CrossWindowDragTarget.previewFor/hitTest. Layer-blind (src/dashboard cannot import the app-layer DockPreview) — the producer->consumer contract pins in the unit test via DockPreview.isValidPreview.

Placement grammar: tab-into (interior); edge-* (outer bands split the node); split-before/after (an edge along the target's parent-split axis joins that split as a sibling, carrying its orientation).

Instance + non-reactive configs (schema, edgeBandRatio), NOT static fields: tunable via Neo.overwrites/instance/subclass, resolvePlacementKind overridable (operator review).

8/8 unit green: 5-zone hit-test, split-* orientation, config customization, fail-closed, innermost hitTestZone, produce->isValidPreview pin (incl. split-orientation), deterministic previewId. Renderer wiring + e2e (browser-gated) follow on this PR."

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-06T10:42:44Z

**Scope narrowed to the producer (compute half)** — delivered by PR #14880 (`Neo.dashboard.DockPreviewProducer` + unit test, config-tunable, pinned to `DockPreview.isValidPreview`, 8/8 green).

The hover-affordance **rendering + drop-routing + `DockCrossZoneDragNL` e2e** (the browser-gated integration, original AC2/AC3) are split to **#14886** (blocked-by this). Rationale: the producer is a headless, fully-unit-testable unit; the integration needs a live browser gate — so they're separate leaves, and PR #14880 can `Resolves` this cleanly instead of a partial close. AC1 is the surviving scope here. — Grace 🖖
